html:
Holcim Elevate Product Lines and What the Rebrand Means

Holcim Elevate is the current name for the commercial roofing business formerly operated as Firestone Building Products, now under Holcim ownership and rebranded as Elevate. Owners who had a roof installed under the Firestone name years ago and are now researching warranty or product questions sometimes don't realize the two names refer to the same underlying product lines and manufacturer history, so we address that directly before getting into spec details.

Legacy Firestone Naming on Older Spartanburg Roofs

If a Spartanburg County building has an EPDM roof installed under the Firestone brand, that warranty and product documentation remains relevant even though the manufacturer now operates as Holcim Elevate. When we inspect an older roof for reroofing or repair, we ask for whatever original warranty paperwork exists, regardless of which brand name appears on it, since the underlying manufacturer relationship carries forward. This comes up often enough on Spartanburg County's older commercial buildings that we've built a standard first step into every roof evaluation: locate and photograph any warranty documents on site, from a filing cabinet or a building maintenance binder, before recommending a scope of work.

Product Categories

Holcim Elevate's commercial catalog includes EPDM membrane in black and white, TPO membrane, and associated insulation and accessory products sold as complete roof assemblies. Their EPDM line has a long history in the commercial roofing market under the Firestone name specifically, and EPDM remains a significant part of their current product offering alongside TPO. The catalog also includes ballast, insulation, and accessory products intended to complete a full assembly, similar to the accessory approach other major manufacturers take with their own membrane lines.

Spec Sheet and Data Verification

As with any manufacturer, we pull the current Holcim Elevate product data sheet for the exact membrane variant being specified, checking thickness, reinforcement, tensile and tear strength, and applicable ASTM references before finalizing a spec for a Spartanburg County project. Product naming and formulation can change over time as a company transitions branding, so working from the current published sheet rather than older Firestone-branded literature matters on any active project.

FM and UL Assembly Ratings

Holcim Elevate maintains FM Approvals and UL listings for tested roof assembly configurations, the same as other major manufacturers. We confirm the applicable assembly number against a project's actual insulation, deck, and fastening plan, since assemblies tested under a prior Firestone product name may carry forward under the current Elevate designation, and verifying the current listing avoids relying on outdated documentation.

  • Ask for original warranty paperwork regardless of whether it shows Firestone or Elevate branding
  • Confirm current product data sheets, since naming and specs have changed with the rebrand
  • Verify the current FM or UL assembly listing rather than relying on older documentation
  • Review current warranty terms and conditions before contract signing
  • Keep updated safety data sheets on file for whichever specific product is installed
  • Confirm with the manufacturer directly if warranty transfer questions arise from a change in building ownership

Warranty Continuity After the Rebrand

Warranty terms issued under the Firestone name generally continue to be honored by Holcim Elevate as the successor manufacturer, though owners with warranty questions on an older roof should confirm current status directly with the manufacturer rather than assuming coverage details haven't changed. We help owners track down that documentation when it's part of evaluating whether a roof still has active coverage before recommending repair, recoat, or replacement. In some cases the manufacturer can locate a warranty record from the original installation date and address even when the physical paperwork has been lost, so we treat a missing document as a starting point for a phone call rather than an automatic assumption that coverage has lapsed.

Regional Application: Hail and Thermal Cycling

The same regional factors that shape our thickness and reinforcement recommendations for any manufacturer's EPDM or TPO apply to Holcim Elevate products: Upstate South Carolina's spring hail activity and humid summer thermal cycling. We reference the manufacturer's current published test data for impact resistance and thermal performance rather than carrying forward assumptions from older Firestone-branded product literature that may not reflect current formulations.

Questions About Holcim Elevate Products

Is my Firestone warranty still valid now that the company is Holcim Elevate?

Warranties issued under Firestone are generally honored by the successor company, but we recommend confirming current status directly with the manufacturer using your original documentation, particularly on older roofs.

Are Firestone and Elevate EPDM the same product?

They come from the same manufacturing lineage, though current Elevate-branded products should be verified against their current data sheets rather than assumed identical to older Firestone literature.

Does Holcim Elevate still make EPDM, or have they moved entirely to TPO?

EPDM remains part of Holcim Elevate's current commercial product catalog alongside TPO and other roofing system offerings.

Who do we contact for warranty transfer on a building sale?

Warranty transfer processes are handled directly through the manufacturer and typically require documentation from the original installation date. We can help gather the necessary paperwork, inspection records, and correspondence needed to support that transfer request during a change in building ownership or a refinancing review.

Does the Holcim ownership change affect product availability in South Carolina?

We haven't seen availability issues on standard product lines, but we confirm current lead times and stock directly with suppliers for each project before finalizing a schedule with the owner.
